region_of_origin: USA (Beyond Meat, Inc.); sold in Singapore at Cold Storage, NTUC FairPrice, and RedMart
food_subgroup: Ultra-processed plant-based burger patty; pea protein isolate + rice protein + mung bean protein blend; soy-free meat analogue

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for the Beyond Burger Patty as a manufactured plant-based meat alternative. Closest parent would be a generic plant-based meat substitute node; URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0MCB is the closest defensible FoodEx2 parent for a manufactured plant-based burger patty. No exact code exists for Beyond Burger specifically.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Beyond Burger patty as sold (113g raw) is the typical single-serve portion, intended to be pan-fried or grilled before eating.

nutrient_source.title: Beyond Burger Patty — Beyond Meat Singapore Official Nutrition Panel
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrition data taken directly from the Beyond Meat Singapore official product page and retail label for the 113g raw patty. Values scaled to per-100g basis. This is a USDA Branded Food category product; the manufacturer's label is the primary approved source for packaged commercial products per Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index estimation for high-protein, high-fat plant-based meat analogue — analogous to low-GI protein-dominant foods
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate basis)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beyond Burger Patty in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~35 is estimated by analogy to protein-and-fat-dominant plant-based foods with minimal available carbohydrate (~4.4g/100g). Available carbohydrate per 113g patty ≈ 4.0g; GL = (35 × 4.0) / 100 ≈ 1.4. This is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database methodology for protein-dominant foods. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.guidance: The Beyond Burger Patty contains approximately 390mg sodium per raw patty, which is moderate-high for a single protein component; hypertensive patients should account for this within a daily sodium budget of 1500–2000mg and avoid adding salt during cooking. Potassium chloride in the formulation (~420mg potassium per 100g) partially offsets the sodium effect on blood pressure, but CKD patients should note the elevated potassium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~35) and very low glycemic load (~1.4 per patty)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of only 1.4 per 113g patty, the Beyond Burger Patty has negligible glycaemic impact and is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes; the high protein content (20g per patty) further supports satiety and post-meal glucose stability, making it a favourable protein choice within a diabetes meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 4.4g/100g (approximately 5g per patty) from coconut oil and cocoa butter; zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Beyond Burger Patty contains approximately 5g saturated fat per patty, primarily from coconut oil and cocoa butter (stearic acid from cocoa butter is considered neutral on LDL); it contains zero cholesterol, which is favourable compared to animal beef, but cardiovascular patients should still moderate saturated fat intake and consider that this is an ultra-processed NOVA Class 4 product with moderate sodium, which may affect overall cardiovascular risk when consumed frequently.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Soy-free formulation; lower saturated fat than Impossible Burger; zero cholesterol
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The Beyond Burger Patty is soy-free (no isoflavone exposure), contains lower saturated fat (~5g) than the Impossible Burger (~8g), and delivers zero cholesterol, making it a more cardiovascularly favourable plant-based protein option for RA patients with concurrent dyslipidaemia.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Beyond Burger Patty is a 113g ultra-processed plant-based meat analogue manufactured by Beyond Meat (USA) and sold in Singapore at Cold Storage, NTUC FairPrice, and RedMart. It is designed to replicate the taste, texture, and appearance of a beef burger patty. The protein matrix is built on pea protein isolate, rice protein, and mung bean protein (PDCAAS approximately 0.92), delivering approximately 20g protein per patty — comparable to lean ground beef. Fat comes from coconut oil, cocoa butter, and canola oil, providing approximately 18g total fat and 5g saturated fat per patty; the stearic acid fraction from cocoa butter is considered neutral on LDL cholesterol. The product is coloured with beet juice extract (not leghemoglobin as used in Impossible Burger). It is SOY-FREE, gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal-certified. Per 113g raw patty: approximately 250 kcal, 20g protein, 18g fat (5g SFA), 5g carbohydrate, 390mg sodium, 0mg cholesterol. GI approximately 35 (low), GL approximately 1.4 — essentially no glycaemic impact. NOVA Class 4 (ultra-processed). Potassium chloride is used as a partial sodium substitute, contributing approximately 420mg potassium per 100g. The patty is intended to be pan-fried or grilled before consumption. Key clinical notes: moderate sodium (390mg/patty) requires monitoring for hypertension; very low GI and GL make it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management; zero cholesterol and lower SFA than Impossible Burger are cardiovascular advantages, though ultra-processed status and moderate sodium warrant moderation. Available at A&W Singapore as the Beyond Burger menu item.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from Beyond Meat Singapore official product label (exact match for a packaged commercial product). GI is an analogous estimate with documented methodology and 0.9 confidence. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are populated from the verified label data.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not reported on the Beyond Meat Singapore retail label and is not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific SKU as of April 2026. 2. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content is not declared on the retail label and is not available in an approved source for this product. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Beyond Meat Singapore official label. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; this is a 100% plant-derived product containing no animal-sourced ingredients. 5. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per label; the product contains no added sugars and negligible intrinsic sugars from plant protein ingredients. 6. Vitamin B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamin A, C, D are all 0.0 per the retail label; these vitamins are not fortified in the current Singapore SKU formulation. 7. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database methodology for protein-and-fat-dominant foods; no direct GI measurement for Beyond Burger Patty was found in the University of Sydney database as of April 2026. GL per 113g patty = (35 × 4.0g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 1.4. 8. This is a NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed food. 9. The product is soy-free, which distinguishes it from the Impossible Burger (which uses soy protein). 10. Tamil local name 'பியாண்ட் பர்கர் பட்டி' (Piyāṇṭ parkar paṭṭi) and Hindi local name 'बियॉन्ड बर्गर पैटी' (Biyond bargar paiti) are transliterations of the brand name into Tamil script and Devanagari script respectively, provided to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Singapore retail products. These are phonetic transliterations as no established Tamil or Hindi vernacular name exists for this US-manufactured packaged food product.
